filters.term: apply_overrides=0 levels=chml rows=1000 min_qod=70 first=1 sort-reverse=severity result_hosts_only=0 notes=1 overrides=1

target.name: Target for immediate scan of IP 192.168.210.132 - 2026-03-24 18:51:23

result.name: Greenbone Security Assistant (GSA) Default Credentials (HTTP)

nvt.name: Greenbone Security Assistant (GSA) Default Credentials (HTTP)
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:C|summary=The remote Greenbone Security Assistant (GSA) is installed / configured in a way that it has account(s) with default passwords enabled.|insight=|affected=|impact=This issue may be exploited by a remote attacker to gain access to sensitive information or modify system configuration.|solution=Change the password of the mentioned account(s).|vuldetect=Tries to login with known default credentials via the HTTP protocol.|solution_type=Workaround
nvt.solution: Change the password of the mentioned account(s).

result.description:
It was possible to login using the following credentials (username:password):
admin:admin

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server /server-status Accessible (HTTP)
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:N|summary=Requesting the URI /server-status provides information on the server activity and performance.|insight=server-status is a Apache HTTP Server handler provided by the 'mod_status' module and used to retrieve the server's activity and performance.|affected=- All Apache installations with an enabled 'mod_status' module
- FreedomBox through 20.13|impact=Requesting the URI /server-status gives throughout information about the currently running Apache to an attacker.|solution=- If this feature is unused commenting out the appropriate section in the web servers configuration is recommended
- If this feature is used restricting access to trusted clients is recommended
- If the FreedomBox software is running on the target update the software to a later version|vuldetect=Checks if the /server-status page of Apache is accessible.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
- If this feature is unused commenting out the appropriate section in the web servers configuration is recommended
- If this feature is used restricting access to trusted clients is recommended
- If the FreedomBox software is running on the target update the software to a later version

result.name: Check if Mailserver answer to VRFY and EXPN requests

nvt.name: Check if Mailserver answer to VRFY and EXPN requests
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:P|summary=The Mailserver on this host answers to VRFY and/or EXPN requests.|insight=VRFY and EXPN ask the server for information about an address. They are inherently unusable through firewalls, gateways, mail exchangers for part-time hosts, etc.|affected=|impact=|solution=Disable VRFY and/or EXPN on your Mailserver.
For postfix add 'disable_vrfy_command=yes' in 'main.cf'.
For Sendmail add the option 'O PrivacyOptions=goaway'.
It is suggested that, if you really want to publish this type of information, you use a mechanism that legitimate users actually know about, such as Finger or HTTP.|vuldetect=|solution_type=Workaround
nvt.solution:
Disable VRFY and/or EXPN on your Mailserver.
For postfix add 'disable_vrfy_command=yes' in 'main.cf'.
For Sendmail add the option 'O PrivacyOptions=goaway'.
It is suggested that, if you really want to publish this type of information, you use a mechanism that legitimate users actually know about, such as Finger or HTTP.

result.description: 'VRFY root' produces the following answer: 252 2.0.0 root

result.name: SSL/TLS: Deprecated TLSv1.0 and TLSv1.1 Protocol Detection

nvt.name: SSL/TLS: Deprecated TLSv1.0 and TLSv1.1 Protocol Detection
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:M/Au:N/C:N/I:P/A:N|summary=It was possible to detect the usage of the deprecated TLSv1.0 and/or TLSv1.1 protocol on this system.|insight=The TLSv1.0 and TLSv1.1 protocols contain known cryptographic flaws like:
- CVE-2011-3389: Browser Exploit Against SSL/TLS (BEAST)
- CVE-2015-0204: Factoring Attack on RSA-EXPORT Keys Padding Oracle On Downgraded Legacy Encryption (FREAK)|affected=- All services providing an encrypted communication using the TLSv1.0 and/or TLSv1.1 protocols
- CVE-2023-41928: Kiloview P1 4G and P2 4G Video Encoder
- CVE-2024-41270: Gorush v1.18.4
- CVE-2025-3200: Multiple products from Wiesemann & Theis|impact=An attacker might be able to use the known cryptographic flaws to eavesdrop the connection between clients and the service to get access to sensitive data transferred within the secured connection.
Furthermore newly uncovered vulnerabilities in this protocols won't receive security updates anymore.|solution=It is recommended to disable the deprecated TLSv1.0 and/or TLSv1.1 protocols in favor of the TLSv1.2+ protocols.
Please see the references for more resources supporting you with this task.|vuldetect=Checks the used TLS protocols of the services provided by this system.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
It is recommended to disable the deprecated TLSv1.0 and/or TLSv1.1 protocols in favor of the TLSv1.2+ protocols.
Please see the references for more resources supporting you with this task.

result.description: In addition to TLSv1.2+ the service is also providing the deprecated TLSv1.0 and TLSv1.1 protocols and supports one or more ciphers. Those supported ciphers can be found in the 'SSL/TLS: Report Supported Cipher Suites' (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.802067) VT.

result.name: SSL/TLS: Deprecated TLSv1.0 and TLSv1.1 Protocol Detection

nvt.name: SSL/TLS: Deprecated TLSv1.0 and TLSv1.1 Protocol Detection
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:M/Au:N/C:N/I:P/A:N|summary=It was possible to detect the usage of the deprecated TLSv1.0 and/or TLSv1.1 protocol on this system.|insight=The TLSv1.0 and TLSv1.1 protocols contain known cryptographic flaws like:
- CVE-2011-3389: Browser Exploit Against SSL/TLS (BEAST)
- CVE-2015-0204: Factoring Attack on RSA-EXPORT Keys Padding Oracle On Downgraded Legacy Encryption (FREAK)|affected=- All services providing an encrypted communication using the TLSv1.0 and/or TLSv1.1 protocols
- CVE-2023-41928: Kiloview P1 4G and P2 4G Video Encoder
- CVE-2024-41270: Gorush v1.18.4
- CVE-2025-3200: Multiple products from Wiesemann & Theis|impact=An attacker might be able to use the known cryptographic flaws to eavesdrop the connection between clients and the service to get access to sensitive data transferred within the secured connection.
Furthermore newly uncovered vulnerabilities in this protocols won't receive security updates anymore.|solution=It is recommended to disable the deprecated TLSv1.0 and/or TLSv1.1 protocols in favor of the TLSv1.2+ protocols.
Please see the references for more resources supporting you with this task.|vuldetect=Checks the used TLS protocols of the services provided by this system.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
It is recommended to disable the deprecated TLSv1.0 and/or TLSv1.1 protocols in favor of the TLSv1.2+ protocols.
Please see the references for more resources supporting you with this task.

result.description: In addition to TLSv1.2+ the service is also providing the deprecated TLSv1.0 and TLSv1.1 protocols and supports one or more ciphers. Those supported ciphers can be found in the 'SSL/TLS: Report Supported Cipher Suites' (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.802067) VT.

result.name: Weak MAC Algorithm(s) Supported (SSH)

nvt.name: Weak MAC Algorithm(s) Supported (SSH)
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:H/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=The remote SSH server is configured to allow / support weak MAC algorithm(s).|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=Disable the reported weak MAC algorithm(s).|vuldetect=Checks the supported MAC algorithms (client-to-server and server-to-client) of the remote SSH server.
Currently weak MAC algorithms are defined as the following:
- MD5 based algorithms
- 96-bit based algorithms
- 64-bit based algorithms
- 'none' algorithm|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution: Disable the reported weak MAC algorithm(s).

result.description:
The remote SSH server supports the following weak client-to-server MAC algorithm(s):
umac-64-etm@openssh.com umac-64@openssh.com
The remote SSH server supports the following weak server-to-client MAC algorithm(s):
umac-64-etm@openssh.com umac-64@openssh.com

detail.name: Services
detail.value: 25,tcp,smtp,A SMTP server is running on this port.

detail.name: Services
detail.value:
22,tcp,ssh,Remote SSH server banner: SSH-2.0-OpenSSH_10.0p2 Debian-7+deb13u1 Remote SSH supported authentication: password,publickey Remote SSH text/login banner: (not available)
This is probably (a):
- OpenSSH
Concluded from remote connection attempt with credentials:

detail.name: SSL/TLS: Collect and Report Certificate Details
